programmes.show_description: Sunset mansions. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie joins the world of show business after meeting Janey and Denzil.
1997. Adventure. Directed by Suri Krishnamma. Stars Greg Wise, Simon Dutton & Dougray Scott. A group of soldiers on a British Army expedition attempt to navigate an unexplored Malaysian gully.
1956. Mystery. Director: Henry Cass. Stars Dale Robertson, Lois Maxwell & Derek Bond. An actress is assisted by an American playwright after her scissors are found lodged in her producer's back.
The Bride Possessed. 1959. Stars Virginia Leith, Skip Homeier & Harry Townes. Matt Conroy is startled when on their honeymoon his wife Sally suddenly is a determined woman, who denies knowing him.
1959. Client: Peter Warren. Western with Peter Breck. After his brothers are killed in a shootout, a gunfighter decides to hang up his guns and uphold the law by becoming a lawyer.
1958. Crime Drama. A reporter on the verge of exposing a drugs ring is killed before he can write the story. Stars Paul Carpenter and Jackie Collins.
Founder of Talking Pictures TV and one half of the Footage Detectives, Noel Cronin, shares shows and films not to be missed throughout March.
1938. Drama. Directed by John Cromwell. Stars Charles Boyer, Hedy Lamarr & Sigrid Gurie. A wanted jewel thief hiding in the Casbah meets a beautiful woman who makes him long for an escape.
1959. Comedy starring Sid James, Brian Rix, Leo Franklyn, Tommy Cooper and Vera Day. The boxing-mad nephew of a pious clergyman embarks on an elaborate plan to keep his boxing career a secret.
1947. Crime Drama. Directed by Brian Desmond Hurst. Stars Eric Portman, Sally Gray & Patrick Holt. A young French girl instigates rivalry between two brothers when she marries the younger one.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Fings Are Getting Smaller
programmes.show_description: 1964. A fascinating look at how everything is being reduced in size to fit in with the new compact age.
1959. Drama. Director: Montgomery Tully. Stars Gordon Jackson, June Thorburn & Mary Clare. Roger forges a new life for himself after his release from prison. But his past catches up with him.
With Menaces - Part 1. 1974. Stars Basil Henson, Jonathan Elsom & Richard Wilson. A landlord is accused of demanding money with menaces from a number of illegal immigrants.
With Menaces - Part 2. 1974. Stars Basil Henson, Jonathan Elsom & Richard Wilson. A fisherman tells the court that the landlord asked him to bring in eight immigrants, but Mr. Aslam never paid.
With Menaces - Part 3. 1974. Stars Basil Henson, Jonathan Elsom & Richard Wilson. Mr. Aslam is forced to explain a two-year jail sentence he served and he admits what the money owed was for.

programmes.show_name: The Happiest Days of Your Life
programmes.show_description: 1950. Comedy. Directed by Frank Launder. Stars Alastair Sim, Margaret Rutherford & John Turnbull. Chaos ensues for staff and students when an all-boys and an all-girls school are merged into one.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Digging Up the Past
programmes.show_description: 1964. A remarkable view of an archaeological dig by both professionals and amateurs to discover ancient Roman remains.
Nella Jones/Millboro. 1994. A look at the story of American - Maureen Williamson, who unravelled the secrets of her past life and psychic detective Nella Jones talks about her amazing powers.
A Family Gathering. 1973. Stars Jean Anderson, Patrick O'Connell & Mark McManus. Mary returns from her recuperation trip abroad. Meanwhile, an offer is put in for land adjoining the Hammond depot.
The Sound of Death. 1958. Stars Donald Gray, Diana Decker & Gilbert Winfield. On his first holiday in two years, Mark Saber finds himself involved in a murder.

programmes.show_name: The Wheeltappers and Shunters Social Club
programmes.show_description: Episode 27. 1975. Sheila Southern performs 'Sing', and Terry Lightfoot and his band perform four numbers. Pat Mooney, Wee Willie Harris and Stu Francis also feature.
1989. Drama. Director: Michael Caton-Jones. Stars John Hurt, Joanne Whalley & Bridget Fonda. An affair between an exotic dancer and the Minister of War shakes up the British government.
Tomorrow the Rat. 1970. Stars John Paul, Simon Oates & Joby Blanshard. A species of genetically engineered rats, with increased intelligence and a thirst for blood, escape into London.
The Gloria Scott. Enjoy this classic short story read by Mike Read in which an old friend of Sherlock's comes to him for help regarding a visit his father received by seaman from his past.
The Woman Least Likely - Part 1. 1974. Stars David Ashford, William Mervyn & William Simons. Irene Rutland is in court facing charges of theft, criminal damage and wounding a police officer.
The Woman Least Likely - Part 2. 1974. Stars Katharine Blake, David Troughton & Sara Clee. Linda Brightson accuses the defendant of taking her handbag but Mrs Rutland denies the charge.
The Woman Least Likely - Part 3. 1974. Stars Griffith Jones, William Marlowe & David Ashford. Bryan Hodge admits that after the death of Irene's husband, she began to drink alcohol after work.
1992. Crime drama with Bernard Hill and Norman Wisdom. Former petty thief and safecracker Arthur is the 'brains' behind 'The Organisation' but he wants out.
1963. Who Killed Purity Mather? Directed Walter Grauman. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. A practicing witch sends Amos a message predicting her murder with a list of five suspects.
1960. Rabbit's Fang. Stars Michael Ansara, Don Dubbins & Constance Ford. Sam becomes involved in a conflict with a gunfighter and a greedy husband.
1954. Directed by Wolf Rilla and starring Jimmy Hanley, Rona Anderson and Lionel Jeffries. A local reporter discovers a group of enemy agents, but will the story get him promotion?
1972. Comedy. Directed by John Robins. Stars Hylda Baker, Jimmy Jewel & Edward Malin. After their father's death, two siblings inherit a decrepit pickle factory and attempt to keep it afloat.
1975. Family. Directed by David Eady. Stars Stephen Garlick, Ray Barrett & Robin Askwith. A group of children find themselves held hostage by two escaped convicts from a nearby prison.
1964. Comedy. Director: Jeremy Summers. Stars Barbara Windsor, Bernard Cribbins, Melvyn Hayes. After pulling off the smallest train robbery, Little Walter and his crew decide to get out of London.
Candidate for Murder. 1961. Directed by David Villiers. Stars Michael Gough, Erika Remberg & John Justin. Kersten, a professional killer, is brought in by a jealous husband to kill his wife.
1962. Crime drama starring Richard Todd, Robert Morley, Dudley Sutton & Jess Conrad. When a nightwatchman is murdered, four young men are put on trial. Cleverly directed by Sidney J. Furie.
It's time to move with Vince Taylor as he performs Whole Lotta Twisting.
Intent to Defraud. 1960. Stars John Ireland, Joseph O'Conor & Philip Latham. When an aging playwright dies of a seizure, Hunter investigates to discover whether it was an accident or a murder.
1961. War. Directed by Ernest Morris. Stars Dermot Walsh & Patrick McAlinney. Major Bell and a ragtag team undertake a dangerous mission to destroy a bridge of strategic importance to the enemy.
1945. In this charming short video Charlie Chester promotes the importance of orange juice for children under five.
1952. Directed by Maclean Rogers and starring John Bentley, Patricia Dainton and Peter Gawthorne. Follows Temple's efforts to aid the police in solving a series of gruesome murders.
Full Remission. 1962. Stars Stratford Johns, Diane Aubrey, Douglas Cummings & Yootha Joyce. Two people are robbed at gun point, Chief Inspector Barlow is suspicious about the cash that was stolen.
Caroline Munro is back this week to introduce the next episode of Supernatural, in which Lady Sybil is seeing ghosts!
Lady Sybil. 1977. Stars Cathleen Nesbitt, Denholm Elliott & John Osborne. Lady Sybil is frightened of a Phantom, she wonders if the ghost of her dead husband haunts the grounds.
Prepare for a night of mad scientists, monsters and murder because next up, Caroline Munro introduces a 1959 horror film: The Manster.
1959. Horror. Director: George P. Breakston. Stars Peter Dyneley, Jane Hylton & Tetsu Nakamura. A journalist is given a mysterious injection that turns him into a murderous monster.
As the end of the night approaches, Caroline Munro is here to give you an insight into what is to come next week at the Cellar Club.

programmes.show_name: Hammer House of Mystery and Suspense
programmes.show_description: Czech Mate. 1984. Stars Susan George, Patrick Mower & Peter Vaughan. Vicky goes to Prague with her ex-husband but soon finds herself alone, penniless and caught up in a murder.
